series_metadata.series_description: These are records concerning academic scholarship awards to prospective and current students. Included are correspondence, applications, recommendations, grade listings, and approvals or disapprovals. These records are recommended to be retained for four years after the recipient has graduated or leaves the school.

retention_rules.trigger_desc: Temporary Record. 4 years recipient has graduated or leaves school

series_metadata.series_description: These files are created when a loan is awarded to a recipient. Included in this file are the student's application, repayment documentation, and any supporting documentation as requested by the board. A loan recipient can repay in two ways. She can provide dental service to an area or repay with their own funds. If the recipient chooses to carry out service in lieu of payment, the board will assign them a service area. The loan recipient may request a specific area in which to provide service to, but these areas must have the approval of the board as well as the Alabama Department of Public Health. The recommended retention for these files is three years after repayment has been completed.

retention_rules.trigger_desc: Temporary Record. 3 years repayment has been completed

series_metadata.series_description: The board creates brochures with information about the scholarship and loans process. These are sent to people who are interested in applying for loans and are interested in merit scholarship opportunities. \** ## Permanent Records List 1. Meeting Minutes and Orders of the Board 1. retention_rules.trigger_desc: Temporary Record. 3 years submission of Fiscal Operations Report that year. ## Requirement and Recommendations Implementing Records Disposition Authority (RDA) Under Code of Alabama 1975 § 41-13-21, "no state officer or agency head shall cause any state record be destroyed or otherwise disposed of without first obtaining approval of State Records Commission." This Records Disposition Authority constitutes authorization by State Records Commission disposition of records of Board of Dental Scholarship Awards (hereafter referred as agency) as stipulated in this document. One condition of this authorization is that agency submit an annual Records Disposition Authority (RDA) Implementation Report on agency records management activities, including documentation of records destruction, State Records Commission in April of each year. In addition, agency should make every effort establish and maintain a quality record-keeping program through following activities: - agency should designate a records liaison, who is responsible : ensuring development of quality record keeping systems that meet business and legal needs of agency, coordinating and destruction of records, ensuring that permanent records held on alternative storage media (such as microforms and digital imaging systems) are maintained in compliance with national and state standards, and ensuring regular implementation of agency's approved RDA. <!-- --> - Permanent records in agency's custody should be maintained under proper intellectual control and in an environment that will ensure their physical order and preservation. - Destruction of temporary records, as authorized in this RDA, should occur agency-wide on a regular basis -- example, successful completion of an audit, at end of an administration, or at end of a fiscal year. Despite RDA's provisions, no record should be destroyed that is necessary comply with requirements of state Sunset Act, audit requirements, or any legal notice or subpoena. - agency should maintain full documentation of any computerized record-keeping system it employs. It should develop procedures : (1) backing up all permanent records held in electronic format; (2) storing a back-up copy off-site; and (3) migrating all permanent records when system is upgraded or replaced. If board chooses maintain permanent records solely in electronic format, it is committed funding any system upgrades and migration strategies necessary ensure records' permanent preservation and accessibility. staff of State Records Commission or Examiners of Public Accounts may examine condition of permanent records maintained in custody of agency and inspect records destruction documentation. Government Records Division archivists are available instruct agency staff in RDA implementation and otherwise assist agency in implementing its records management program. State Records Commission adopted this Records Disposition Authority on April 18, 2003. \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_ \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_ Edwin C.
